walkthrough.title: Separate the Baby Unicorns
walkthrough.text: Let's get this magical mayhem started! First, find all the Baby Unicorn cards in the deck. These have a distinct baby unicorn design and are separate from the main deck. Pull them out and place them face up in the center of the table. This pile is called the Nursery. Baby Unicorns never go into the main deck.

walkthrough.title: Each Player Takes a Baby Unicorn
walkthrough.text: Every player picks one Baby Unicorn from the Nursery and places it face up in front of them. This is the start of your stable, your personal play area where your unicorns and other cards will live. Your Baby Unicorn is your first unicorn and counts toward your total. Return any unchosen Baby Unicorns to the Nursery.

walkthrough.title: Shuffle and Deal the Main Deck
walkthrough.text: Take all the remaining cards, which include Basic Unicorns, Magical Unicorns, Magic cards, Instant cards, Upgrades, and Downgrades. Shuffle them thoroughly and deal five cards face down to each player. This is your starting hand. Keep it secret from other players.

walkthrough.title: Create the Draw and Discard Piles
walkthrough.text: Place the rest of the shuffled deck face down in the center of the table. This is the draw pile. Leave space next to it for a face-up discard pile that will form during play. The Nursery, draw pile, and discard pile should all be within easy reach of every player.

walkthrough.title: Set Up Your Stable Area
walkthrough.text: Each player needs clear table space in front of them for their stable. Your stable will hold your unicorns, any Upgrade cards that benefit you, and any Downgrade cards that opponents play on you. Keep your stable organized so everyone can count your unicorns at a glance. Visibility matters because everyone needs to track who is closest to winning.

walkthrough.title: Determine Win Condition and First Player
walkthrough.text: Check your player count. With two to five players, you need seven unicorns in your stable to win. With six to eight players, you only need six. The person whose birthday is closest to today goes first. Play proceeds clockwise. Everyone starts with five cards in hand and one Baby Unicorn in their stable. Let the unicorn chaos begin!

bullets: Pull all Baby Unicorn cards from the deck
Place face up in center of table = the Nursery

bullets: Pick 1 Baby Unicorn → place face up in your stable

bullets: Shuffle all remaining cards together

bullets: Remaining deck = face-down draw pile in center
Leave space for face-up discard pile

summary.title: Set Up Your Stable Area

Holds unicorns, Upgrades, and Downgrades — keep organized

summary.title: Determine Win Condition and First Player
bullets: 2-5 players = 7 unicorns to win; 6-8 players = 6 unicorns
Closest birthday goes first → clockwise
Start: 5 cards in hand, 1 Baby Unicorn in stable

walkthrough.title: Game Overview
walkthrough.text: Unstable Unicorns is a race to fill your stable with unicorns. Build up your herd while sabotaging your opponents with destructive Magic cards and annoying Downgrades. The first player to reach seven unicorns in their stable (or six with more players) at the beginning of their turn wins. It sounds cute, but this game gets cutthroat fast.

walkthrough.title: Turn Structure: Four Phases
walkthrough.text: Each turn has four phases in order. Phase one is the Beginning of Turn, where triggered effects resolve. Phase two is the Draw Phase, where you draw one card. Phase three is the Action Phase, where you play one card or draw an extra card. Phase four is the End of Turn, where end-of-turn effects resolve and you check the hand limit. Let's break down each phase.

walkthrough.title: Phase One: Beginning of Turn
walkthrough.text: At the start of your turn, resolve any effects that trigger at the beginning of your turn. Some Magical Unicorns and Upgrade cards have abilities that say something like if this card is in your stable at the beginning of your turn. This is also when the win condition is checked. If you have seven or more unicorns right now, you win!

walkthrough.title: Phase Two: Draw Phase
walkthrough.text: Draw one card from the draw pile and add it to your hand. This is mandatory every turn. If the draw pile is empty, shuffle the discard pile to form a new draw pile. Simple as that. You always get at least one new card per turn.

walkthrough.title: Phase Three: Action Phase
walkthrough.text: This is the core of your turn. You may either play one card from your hand or draw one additional card from the deck. Playing a card means placing a Unicorn into your stable, playing a Magic card for its effect, adding an Upgrade to your stable, or placing a Downgrade in an opponent's stable. If nothing in your hand is useful right now, just draw an extra card instead.

walkthrough.title: Card Types: Unicorns
walkthrough.text: There are two types of unicorns. Basic Unicorns are simple cards with no special abilities. They go into your stable and count toward your total. Magical Unicorns also go into your stable and count toward your total, but they have special effects. Some trigger when they enter your stable, some have passive ongoing abilities, and some activate under specific conditions.

walkthrough.title: Card Types: Magic, Upgrades, and Downgrades
walkthrough.text: Magic cards are one-time effects. Play them, resolve the effect, then discard them. Effects include destroying unicorns, stealing unicorns, returning cards to hands, and more. Upgrade cards go into your stable and provide ongoing benefits like extra actions or protection. Downgrade cards go into an opponent's stable and impose penalties like preventing unicorn play or forcing discards.

walkthrough.title: Instant Cards: Neigh!
walkthrough.text: Neigh cards are the only cards played outside your Action Phase. When any player plays a card, you can shout Neigh and play a Neigh card to cancel it completely. The negated card goes to the discard pile without resolving. A Super Neigh works the same way but cannot itself be Neighed. Neigh chains can happen: a Neigh can be Neighed, which can be Neighed again. The last Neigh in the chain determines the outcome.

walkthrough.title: Phase Four: End of Turn
walkthrough.text: Resolve any end-of-turn effects from cards in your stable. Then check your hand size. If you have more than seven cards in hand, you must discard down to seven. Choose which cards to discard. Then pass the turn to the next player clockwise.

walkthrough.title: Baby Unicorn Rules
walkthrough.text: Baby Unicorns live in the Nursery and cannot be played from your hand. They only enter stables through card effects that specifically bring a Baby Unicorn from the Nursery. If a Baby Unicorn is destroyed or returned, it goes back to the Nursery, not the discard pile. Baby Unicorns count toward your unicorn total just like any other unicorn.

walkthrough.title: Sacrifice, Destroy, and Return
walkthrough.text: These three terms have specific meanings. Sacrifice means you send your own card to the discard pile, usually as a cost for an effect. Destroy means a card effect removes a card from any stable to the discard pile. Return means a card goes back to its owner's hand. Baby Unicorns return to the Nursery instead. Understanding these distinctions matters for resolving card effects correctly.

walkthrough.title: Winning the Game
walkthrough.text: The win condition is checked at the beginning of each player's turn. If you have seven or more unicorns in your stable at that moment, you win. Only Unicorn cards count, both Basic and Magical. Upgrade and Downgrade cards in your stable do not count. If you reach seven unicorns during someone else's turn, you do not win yet. Your opponents have until your next turn to reduce your stable.

summary.title: Game Overview
bullets: Race to 7 unicorns in your stable (6 with 6-8 players)
Build your herd while sabotaging opponents

summary.title: Turn Structure: Four Phases
bullets: 1. Beginning of Turn (triggers + win check)
2. Draw Phase (draw 1)
3. Action Phase (play 1 card OR draw 1 extra)
4. End of Turn (triggers + hand limit check)

walkthrough.title: Welcome to the Stable
walkthrough.text: Welcome to your first game of Unstable Unicorns! We are going to walk through the opening rounds of a five-player game. Our players are Luna, Max, Ivy, Cole, and Zara. Each player has one Baby Unicorn in their stable and five cards in hand. The win condition is seven unicorns. Follow along even if your group has a different number of players.

walkthrough.title: Luna's First Turn
walkthrough.text: Luna checks her hand and sees a Basic Unicorn, a Magical Unicorn called Rhinocorn, a Neigh card, a Magic card, and an Upgrade card. She draws one card in the Draw Phase, getting another Basic Unicorn. For her Action Phase, Luna plays the Basic Unicorn into her stable. She now has two unicorns: her Baby Unicorn and the Basic Unicorn. A solid, simple start.

walkthrough.title: Max Plays an Upgrade Early
walkthrough.text: Max draws his card, then plays an Upgrade card called Extra Tail into his stable. This Upgrade lets him draw an extra card at the start of each turn. No one plays a Neigh. Getting Upgrades out early is smart because they compound over time. Max will have a card advantage every turn for the rest of the game unless someone destroys this Upgrade.

walkthrough.title: Ivy Plays a Downgrade on Max
walkthrough.text: Ivy sees that Max's Upgrade could be dangerous. She draws her card, then plays a Downgrade card called Tiny Stable into Max's stable. Tiny Stable limits the number of unicorns Max can have. Ivy used her action to slow down a potential threat instead of building her own stable. This is a legitimate strategy, especially against players with powerful ongoing effects.

walkthrough.title: Mistake One: Wasting a Neigh Card
walkthrough.text: Cole draws his card. Luna plays her next unicorn on her turn. Cole immediately plays Neigh, canceling Luna's Basic Unicorn. But Luna was only at two unicorns and this is early in the game. That Basic Unicorn was not a threat. Cole just spent his only Neigh card on a low-impact play and now has no defense against game-changing moves later.

walkthrough.title: Lesson: Save Neigh Cards for Big Moments
walkthrough.text: Neigh cards are your most powerful defensive tool. Do not waste them on Basic Unicorns or minor plays early in the game. Save your Neigh for cards that would win the game, devastate your position, or create an unstoppable combo. A well-timed Neigh on someone's seventh unicorn is worth ten times more than stopping a second unicorn from being played.

walkthrough.title: Zara Builds Quietly
walkthrough.text: Zara plays a Basic Unicorn into her stable without drawing attention. She has two unicorns now, same as Luna, but no one is focused on her. Zara also has two Neigh cards and a powerful Magical Unicorn in her hand. She is building resources quietly while others clash. This patient approach keeps targets off your back in the early game.

walkthrough.title: Round Two: The Race Picks Up
walkthrough.text: Luna plays Rhinocorn, a Magical Unicorn with a destroy effect. When Rhinocorn enters her stable, she gets to destroy one unicorn in any player's stable. She targets Max's Baby Unicorn, dropping him to zero unicorns. Luna now has three unicorns and is pulling ahead. Magical Unicorns with enter effects are some of the most powerful cards in the game.

walkthrough.title: Mistake Two: Ignoring the Leader
walkthrough.text: Two rounds later, Luna has five unicorns and no one has acted against her. Max is focused on rebuilding his stable. Ivy is playing more Downgrades. Cole has no Neigh cards left. Zara has been waiting. Luna plays her sixth unicorn and only needs one more to win. Suddenly everyone realizes they should have been stopping Luna turns ago. The table ignored the leader until it was almost too late.

walkthrough.title: Lesson: Always Watch the Unicorn Counts
walkthrough.text: Keep track of how many unicorns each player has at all times. When someone reaches four or five, the table needs to respond with destruction effects, Downgrades, or Neigh cards. If you let one player quietly build to six unicorns, you may not have enough actions left to stop them. Coordinate informally with other players. The leader should always face resistance.

walkthrough.title: Zara Springs Her Trap
walkthrough.text: Zara has been patient. She has five cards in hand including two Neigh cards and a powerful Magical Unicorn. She plays a Magic card that lets her steal one of Luna's unicorns. Then on her next turn, she plays her Magical Unicorn into her stable. When Max tries to Neigh it, Zara counters with her own Neigh. Zara jumps from three unicorns to five in two turns. Patience and hand management pay off.

walkthrough.title: Mistake Three: Miscounting Unicorns
walkthrough.text: Cole looks at his stable and thinks he has five unicorns. But one of those cards is actually an Upgrade, not a unicorn. He plays what he thinks is his sixth unicorn, but he actually only has five. Cole wasted his action pushing for a win that was further away than he thought. Only Unicorn cards count. Upgrades and Downgrades in your stable are not unicorns.

walkthrough.title: Lesson: Count Carefully
walkthrough.text: Only cards labeled as Unicorns count toward your total. This includes Baby Unicorns, Basic Unicorns, and Magical Unicorns. Upgrade and Downgrade cards sit in your stable but do not count. Before planning your winning push, physically count your unicorn cards. It takes two seconds and prevents embarrassing miscounts that waste critical turns.

walkthrough.title: The Final Push
walkthrough.text: The game reaches its climax. Luna has been knocked down to four unicorns. Zara has five and plays a card that brings a Baby Unicorn from the Nursery, reaching six. No one has a Neigh card. On Zara's next turn, she starts with six unicorns. She draws her card, checks her hand, and plays a Basic Unicorn for number seven. The win condition is met at the beginning of her turn. Zara wins!

walkthrough.title: Congratulations
walkthrough.text: Congratulations! You have just experienced the wild swings and strategic depth of Unstable Unicorns. You learned that Neigh cards should be saved for critical moments, the table must always watch unicorn counts and respond to leaders, and miscounting your stable can cost you the game. The balance of building your own stable while disrupting others is what makes this game so much fun.

walkthrough.title: Saddle Up
walkthrough.text:
Before you start your own game, here are three things that can help.

First, check out General Tips for strategies on hand management and Neigh card timing.

Second, reference the Appendix for a quick guide to all card types, timing rules, and win conditions.

And most importantly, ask your personal GM Guide for help anytime. Just tap the Q&A tab and ask any question during your game. Time to build your unicorn army!

summary.title: Saddle Up

Reference the Appendix → card types, timing rules, win conditions
Ask your personal GM Guide → tap Q&A for instant answers during play

walkthrough.title: Build Quietly in the Early Game
walkthrough.text: Resist the urge to play your most powerful cards immediately. If you jump to four or five unicorns early, every player at the table will target you with destruction effects and Downgrades. Instead, play Basic Unicorns and build steadily. Keep your powerful Magical Unicorns and Magic cards in hand for a late-game surge when opponents have fewer resources to stop you.

walkthrough.title: Save Neigh Cards for Critical Plays
walkthrough.text: Neigh cards are your most valuable defensive resource. Do not waste them on Basic Unicorns or minor effects in the early game. Save them for moments that matter: when someone plays their seventh unicorn, when a devastating Magic card would wreck your stable, or when a Magical Unicorn combo would create an unstoppable advantage. One well-timed Neigh can change the entire outcome of the game.

walkthrough.title: Play Upgrades Early, Downgrades Strategically
walkthrough.text: Upgrade cards that give you extra draws or extra actions become more valuable the longer they stay in play. Get them out early to maximize their benefit. Downgrade cards should target the player who is furthest ahead or who has the most dangerous combo potential. Do not spread Downgrades randomly. Focus them on the biggest threat at the table.

walkthrough.title: Track Everyone's Unicorn Count
walkthrough.text: Always know how many unicorns each player has. When someone reaches five unicorns, they are two turns away from winning. The table needs to coordinate responses. Use destruction effects, steal effects, and Neigh cards to keep the leader in check. If you focus only on your own stable and ignore the scoreboard, someone will sneak past you to victory.

walkthrough.title: Manage Your Hand Size
walkthrough.text: The hand limit is seven cards. If you end your turn with more than seven, you must discard. Plan your plays so you stay at or below seven. Sometimes it is better to play a mediocre card than to draw an extra card that pushes you over the limit and forces you to discard something valuable. If you are at six cards and draw for your turn, you will be at seven with room for your action draw.

walkthrough.title: Use Baby Unicorn Effects
walkthrough.text: Cards that bring Baby Unicorns from the Nursery into your stable are surprisingly powerful. They add to your unicorn count without costing a card from your hand. If you have a card that brings in a Baby Unicorn, it is essentially a free unicorn. These effects are often underrated by beginners but can be the difference between six and seven unicorns when you need it most.

walkthrough.title: Time Your Winning Push
walkthrough.text: The ideal time to surge from four or five unicorns to seven is when opponents are out of Neigh cards, distracted by fighting each other, or have depleted their destruction effects. Watch for the moment when the table is weakest. Play two or three cards across two turns to jump to the win condition before anyone can respond. Speed and timing beat raw power.

walkthrough.title: Common Mistakes to Avoid
walkthrough.text: Here are the biggest beginner mistakes in one place. Using Neigh cards on low-impact plays early in the game. Ignoring the leader until they are one unicorn from winning. Miscounting your stable by including Upgrades and Downgrades. Hoarding cards past the seven-card hand limit and being forced to discard good ones. And overcommitting to a single combo strategy that falls apart when one piece gets Neighed or destroyed.

summary.title: Build Quietly in the Early Game
bullets: Play Basic Unicorns first — save powerful cards for later
Racing ahead early makes you the target

summary.title: Save Neigh Cards for Critical Plays
bullets: Don't Neigh early low-impact cards
Save for 7th unicorn plays, devastating combos, or stable-wrecking Magic

summary.title: Play Upgrades Early, Downgrades Strategically
bullets: Upgrades compound over time — get them out ASAP
Downgrades → target the leader or biggest threat

summary.title: Track Everyone's Unicorn Count
bullets: 5 unicorns = 2 turns from winning — table must respond
Coordinate destruction and Neigh cards against the leader

summary.title: Manage Your Hand Size
bullets: Hand limit = 7 cards; plan plays to stay under
Playing a mediocre card > forced discard of a good one

summary.title: Use Baby Unicorn Effects
bullets: Cards bringing Baby Unicorns from Nursery = free unicorns
Don't cost a card from hand — often underrated by beginners

summary.title: Time Your Winning Push
bullets: Surge from 4-5 to 7 when opponents lack Neigh cards
Wait for the table to be distracted or depleted

summary.title: Common Mistakes to Avoid
bullets: Neighing low-impact plays early
Ignoring the leader until it's too late
Miscounting stable (Upgrades/Downgrades aren't unicorns)
Overcommitting to 1 combo that falls apart

walkthrough.title: The Patience Strategy
walkthrough.text: Advanced players often focus the first three or four turns entirely on drawing cards and playing low-key Upgrades. This builds a massive hand with multiple options while keeping your unicorn count low enough that nobody targets you. When you are ready, unleash a rapid sequence of plays over two or three turns to surge from three unicorns to seven before the table can react. Patience wins more games than aggression.

walkthrough.title: Neigh Card Economy
walkthrough.text: Think of Neigh cards as a shared resource at the table. Every player can hold Neigh cards, and everyone knows approximately how many are left in the deck. When the table is collectively low on Neigh cards, that is when you make your winning push. Track which players have used their Neigh cards already. If the two players after you in turn order have both played Neigh recently, they probably cannot stop you.

walkthrough.title: Target Selection: Destroy the Right Thing
walkthrough.text: When you play a destruction effect, do not just destroy any random unicorn. Target the Magical Unicorn with the strongest ongoing ability. A Basic Unicorn is easily replaced, but a Magical Unicorn with a powerful passive effect is much harder to recreate. Similarly, when you can destroy Upgrades, target the ones that provide extra actions or card draws because those create cascading advantages every turn.

walkthrough.title: The Steal and Swing
walkthrough.text: Steal effects are the most powerful cards in the game because they shift the count by two: minus one for the victim, plus one for you. A well-timed steal can swing the game dramatically. If you are at five unicorns and an opponent is at five, stealing from them puts you at six and them at four, a four-unicorn swing. Save steal cards for maximum impact near the end of the game.

walkthrough.title: King-Making Awareness
walkthrough.text: In multiplayer Unstable Unicorns, your actions affect everyone. Destroying one player's unicorns while ignoring another can hand the game to the wrong person. Before targeting someone, scan the whole table. Ask yourself who benefits most if you weaken this particular player. Spread your disruption across multiple threats rather than hammering one player while another quietly builds to seven.

walkthrough.title: Combo Awareness
walkthrough.text: Certain Magical Unicorns create powerful combos with specific Upgrades or other Magical Unicorns. Learn to recognize these combos in your hand and plan multiple turns ahead to deploy them. Equally important: learn to recognize when an opponent is building toward a combo and Neigh the key piece. Denying a combo is often more valuable than building your own stable by one unicorn.

walkthrough.title: Bluffing With Neigh Threats
walkthrough.text: Even when you do not have a Neigh card, acting like you do can influence the table. If you openly comment on watching a player's next move or make a knowing look when someone reaches for their hand, they might hesitate to play their best card. This psychological pressure is free and can delay an opponent's winning push by one or two turns, which is sometimes all you need.

walkthrough.title: Late-Game Hand Management
walkthrough.text: In the late game, every card in your hand matters. Stop drawing extra cards and start playing aggressively. If you have four unicorns and need three more, map out exactly how you will get there. Do you have enough unicorn cards? Do you need a steal? Can you bring a Baby Unicorn from the Nursery? Count your path to seven and execute it over the minimum number of turns. Efficiency in the endgame is everything.

summary.title: The Patience Strategy
bullets: First 3-4 turns: draw cards and play low-key Upgrades
Surge from 3 to 7 unicorns in 2-3 turns when ready

summary.title: Neigh Card Economy
bullets: Track who has used Neigh cards recently
Push for the win when the table is low on Neigh

summary.title: Target Selection: Destroy the Right Thing
bullets: Destroy Magical Unicorns with strong passives > Basic Unicorns
Target Upgrades that give extra actions or draws

summary.title: The Steal and Swing
bullets: Steal = -1 for victim, +1 for you = 2-unicorn swing
Save steal cards for maximum impact near endgame

summary.title: King-Making Awareness
bullets: Scan the whole table before targeting anyone
Spread disruption across threats — don't create a silent winner

summary.title: Combo Awareness
bullets: Learn Magical Unicorn + Upgrade combos in your hand
Neigh the key combo piece of an opponent > building your own stable

summary.title: Bluffing With Neigh Threats
bullets: Act like you have a Neigh card even when you don't
Psychological pressure can delay opponents' winning push

summary.title: Late-Game Hand Management
bullets: Map your exact path to 7 unicorns — count needed cards
Stop drawing, start playing aggressively in endgame

entries.definition: Lives in the Nursery, not the deck. Enters stables only through card effects. Returns to Nursery (not discard) if destroyed or returned. Counts toward unicorn total.
Simple unicorn with no special abilities. Placed in your stable during the Action Phase. Counts toward your unicorn total.
Unicorn with a special ability (enter effect, passive, or triggered). Placed in your stable during the Action Phase. Counts toward your unicorn total.
One-time use card played during your Action Phase. Resolve the effect (destroy, steal, return, search, etc.) then discard. Does not stay in your stable.
Ongoing beneficial effect placed in YOUR stable. Provides bonuses like extra actions, protection, or extra draws. Does not count as a unicorn.
Ongoing negative effect placed in an OPPONENT's stable. Imposes penalties like limiting unicorn count or forcing discards. Does not count as a unicorn.
Instant card played at any time to cancel another player's card as it is played. The negated card goes to discard without resolving. Can be Neighed by another player (Neigh chain).
Instant card that works like Neigh but cannot itself be Neighed. Ends any Neigh chain immediately.
Central face-up pile where Baby Unicorns reside. Baby Unicorns enter stables from here via card effects and return here when destroyed or returned.
Each player's personal play area. Contains their unicorns, Upgrades, and any Downgrades placed by opponents. Only unicorn cards in the stable count toward the win condition.
Face-down central deck. Draw 1 card per turn (mandatory) plus optional extra draw during Action Phase. Reshuffle discard pile when empty.
Send your own card from your stable to the discard pile. Usually required as a cost to activate a card effect. You choose which of your cards to sacrifice.
A card effect removes a card from any player's stable to the discard pile. The card's owner does not choose — the effect determines the target.
A card goes back to its owner's hand. Baby Unicorns return to the Nursery instead. Useful for reusing enter effects on Magical Unicorns.
Ability on a Magical Unicorn that triggers once immediately when the card enters your stable. Example: 'When this card enters your stable, destroy a unicorn.'
Ongoing ability on a card that is always active as long as the card is in your stable. Example: 'Your unicorns cannot be destroyed.'
Effect that activates at the start of your turn (Phase 1). Win condition is also checked at this time. Example: 'At the beginning of your turn, draw an extra card.'
Maximum of 7 cards in hand at end of turn. Excess must be discarded. Plan plays to avoid forced discards of valuable cards.
7 unicorns in stable (2-5 players) or 6 unicorns (6-8 players), checked at beginning of your turn. Only Unicorn cards count — not Upgrades or Downgrades.
When a Neigh is played, another player can Neigh the Neigh, and so on. The last Neigh in the chain determines whether the original card resolves or is canceled.
